h1: Urethane Casting vs. Injection Molding — A DFM Guide

headings.text: Urethane Casting vs. Injection Molding — A DFM Guide
Top DFM Concerns for Urethane Casting and Injection Molding
How to Make Data Work for Mold Productivity and Performance
Breaking Down the Costs of 3D Printing

meta_description: Design for manufacturing (DFM) involves optimizing part design so manufacturers can fabricate high-quality parts with the lowest possible cost-per-unit. By taking the manufacturing method and its restrictions into account, designers can reduce production time, cut costs, and prevent extensive redesigns.

sections.heading: Urethane Casting vs. Injection Molding — A DFM Guide
sections.text: Urethane Casting vs. Injection Molding — A DFM Guide Published on January 10, 2022 Originally published on fastradius.com on January 10, 2022 Design for manufacturability (DFM) involves optimizing part design so manufacturers can fabricate high-quality parts with the lowest possible cost-per-unit. By taking the manufacturing method and its restrictions into account, designers can reduce production time, cut costs, and prevent extensive redesigns. DFM best practices vary depending on the product you’re making and your chosen manufacturing method. While injection molding and urethane casting are both molding technologies that can create molded plastic parts, they have different DFM guidelines...
